content.text: Answer four problems. (If you turn in more, the first four will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Give an example of each of the following, with some explanation.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: A nonabelian group, all of whose subgroups are normal.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: A group \(G\) and a normal subgroup \(N\) of \(G\) such that \(N\) is not a characteristic subgroup of \(G\).

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a group. Prove that the following are equivalent:

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: \(G\) is not equal to the union of its proper subgroups.

problem.text: Let \(S_n\) denote the symmetric group on \(n\) symbols.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that your definition from (a) is independent of any choices that need to be made.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Use your definition from (a) to prove that \(\epsilon(\sigma \circ \tau)=\epsilon(\sigma)\epsilon(\tau)\) for all \(\sigma,\tau\in S_n\).

problem.text:
Let \(G\) be a nontrivial finite \(p\)-group, where \(p\) is a prime, and let \(H\) be the subgroup of \(G\) generated by the set
\[
\{[x,y]:x,y\in G\}\cup\{x^p:x\in G\}.
\]
Prove that \(H\) is a proper normal subgroup of \(G\).
Let \(G\) be a finite group of order \(pqr\), where \(p\), \(q\) and \(r\) are primes and \(p<q<r\). Prove that \(G\) has a normal non-trivial Sylow subgroup.

For each homomorphism \(\phi:\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z}\to\operatorname{Aut}(\mathbb{Z}/8\mathbb{Z})\) describe the semidirect product \((\mathbb{Z}/8\mathbb{Z})\rtimes_\phi(\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z})\). Prove that these groups are pairwise nonisomorphic.
